verses.text: The blessed man refuses the counsel, path, and seat of the ungodly, and instead delights in God's law, meditating day and night; he is like a tree planted by rivers, fruitful and unwithering. His secret is a heart fed on Scripture. To dwell in God's word is to draw life from an unfailing spring.

The blessed man refuses the counsel, path, and seat of the ungodly, and instead delights in God's law, meditating day and night; he is like a tree planted by rivers, fruitful and unwithering. His secret is a heart fed on Scripture. To dwell in God's word is to draw life from an unfailing spring.

The blessed man refuses the counsel, path, and seat of the ungodly, and instead delights in God's law, meditating day and night; he is like a tree planted by rivers, fruitful and unwithering. His secret is a heart fed on Scripture. To dwell in God's word is to draw life from an unfailing spring.

Not so the ungodly: they are like chaff the wind drives away, with no standing in the judgment, for 'the LORD knoweth the way of the righteous,' but the way of the wicked shall perish. Two paths, two ends. God Himself watches over the one and lets the other come to nothing.

Not so the ungodly: they are like chaff the wind drives away, with no standing in the judgment, for 'the LORD knoweth the way of the righteous,' but the way of the wicked shall perish. Two paths, two ends. God Himself watches over the one and lets the other come to nothing.

Not so the ungodly: they are like chaff the wind drives away, with no standing in the judgment, for 'the LORD knoweth the way of the righteous,' but the way of the wicked shall perish. Two paths, two ends. God Himself watches over the one and lets the other come to nothing.
